critique: Strong example because architecture aliases produce stable service and edge IDs; grouped source/markdown reveal avoids overlapping fixtures.
Uses stable rendered block IDs and one connection ID; it intentionally avoids role:edge for precise connector movement.
Good for box-level orchestration, but C4 relationship selection is still coarse because Mermaid renders all relation lines as one candidate.
Uses exact class and relation IDs, which is safer than substring text matching for class names.
Strong current-language example: exact entity and relationship IDs give stable anchors and path tracing.
Shows the right workaround for missing per-edge selectors: keep swimlanes static and animate causality with marks.
Readable node selectors work well; broad edge tracing remains the main limitation until edge:A->B exists.
Preserves date geometry by moving a mark rather than moving task bars.
Uses stable commit label text for point anchors and avoids hash-like generated classes; some commit classes resolve both outer and inner shapes.
Branch labels are easy to select by text, but exact per-branch line selection still needs future cause/branch syntax.
Uses stable card and column IDs; movement is carried by an overlay mark so the board layout remains true.
Combines readable node text with explicit generated edge IDs, which is the safest current branch-level pattern.
Uses full row text plus absolute field centers because packet fields have no semantic IDs yet.
Segment classes are compact, but each category selector intentionally affects wedge, percentage, and legend together.
Uses text labels for exact point selection; plot-space selectors remain future syntax.
Series-level CSS selectors work; per-axis and per-vertex domain selectors remain missing.
Exact requirement and relation IDs make this a good candidate for current directive syntax, but edge direction must be taken from --list-elements.
Uses node IDs and all-flow tracing; precise individual link syntax is still a gap.
Uses broad message tracing and explicit points, because current sequence SVG does not expose semantic message selectors.
Demonstrates dwell with marks and color; future wait/dwell verbs would reduce directive count.
Uses visible timeline labels and generated timeline classes; dense timelines need shorter gaps.
Label selectors work for readable sections/leaves; tiny hidden leaves are intentionally not emphasized.
Labels are exact text targets; branch lines are only emphasized as a group because text-selected folder candidates can overlap line containers.
Uses exact task/section text; no stable IDs are emitted, so broad column classes remain group-only tools.
Generated .venn-sets-* classes are strong current syntax for set/intersection choreography.
Uses text labels for components and xy points for precise symbols because component IDs are absent.
Uses chart-level roles and xy points because individual bars and plot-domain points are not addressable yet.
Relies on explicit xy points and broad item/message groups because data-participant attributes are not selectable yet.
